title: Payton Tolle’s emergence has been a bright spot amid Red Sox’ uneven 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Rookie pitcher Payton Tolle dominated with six scoreless innings in the Red Sox's 8-1 win over Baltimore.
Tolle has a 2.28 ERA in eight starts this season, with five quality outings and only 11 total starts for Boston.
Tolle is the first Red Sox pitcher since 2018 to begin a season with eight starts allowing three or fewer runs.

title: Police investigating after $30,000 in landscaping equipment stolen from community college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two suspects allegedly stole $30,000 in landscaping equipment from Massasoit Community College in Brockton over two days.
The suspects used a stolen silver Ford F-150 truck, taken from a Brockton home on May 23, for their getaway.
Police request information from the public; contact Sergeant Christopher Trucchi at 508-588-9100, ext. 1037, or State Police at 508-894-2600.

title: Hull police prepare for planned ‘large gathering’ at Nantasket Beach
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Hull police will increase staff at Nantasket Beach due to a potential large gathering flagged by social media posts.
Police Chief John Dunn emphasized zero tolerance for illegal activities, disorderly behavior, and violations at the beach.
Recent disturbances at New England beaches include violence at Narragansett and Hampton Beach, prompting increased security measures.

title: American flag burned, then replaced outside Masonic temple in Maine, fire officials says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Fire officials determined that an American flag at a Masonic temple in Bath, Maine, was intentionally burned.
A replacement flag was zip-tied to the temple entrance railing before fire crews arrived on the scene.
Investigators are searching for those responsible for the arson and the person who replaced the flag.

title: Family of Mass. worker who died after getting trapped in freezer gets nearly $13 million in settlement
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Massachusetts worker's family received a nearly $13 million settlement after his death in a freezer incident.
The worker's children filed a wrongful death lawsuit, citing a defective freezer door handle as central to the case.
The settlement was reached through mediation following extensive legal investigations and expert testimonies.

title: Clover plans to reopen some locations after sudden closure, thanks to an anonymous investor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Clover Food Lab will reopen its Boston and Cambridge locations on June 9 after securing an investment deal.
CEO Julia Wrin Piper announced the reopening following an anonymous investor deal, focusing on reducing the chain's footprint.
The vegetarian chain cited inflation and financial pressures for initial closures; future plans include focusing on core community locations.

title: Abreu has a two-run homer and 3 RBIs, Tolle pitches 6 scoreless innings and Red Sox beat Orioles 8-1
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched six scoreless innings, allowing seven hits and striking out five, as the Red Sox beat the Orioles 8-1.
Wilyer Abreu hit a two-run homer and drove in three runs contributing to the Red Sox's offensive success.
Mickey Gasper hit a two-run triple in the fifth inning, part of a five-run rally for the Red Sox.

title: MassDems accuse GOP candidates of intentionally submitting false signatures to make primary ballot
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts Democrats accused Republican candidates Mike Walsh and Anne Manning-Martin of submitting fraudulent signatures for the September primary ballot.
Formal objections were placed citing signatures not signed in person, duplicate signatures, and non-registered voter signatures.
Chair Stephen Kerrigan urged Walsh and Manning-Martin to withdraw, suggesting failure to do so undermines electoral integrity.

title: Isiah Kiner-Falefa, Chad Tracy elaborate on cryptic comments on Red Sox’ Fenway struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Isiah Kiner-Falefa commented on distractions affecting Boston Red Sox's 9-20 record at Fenway Park this season.
Chad Tracy agreed with Kiner-Falefa, noting increased media and staff presence as potential distractions at Fenway.
Tracy emphasized no changes in preparation are planned, attributing the need for improvement to securing more wins.

title: Teen found dismembered in Chelsea parking lot is identified 26 years later
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tiffany Bradley, found dismembered in Chelsea in 2000, was identified 26 years later using DNA technology.
Eugene McCollom was charged with murder in 2001, having confessed and led police to Bradley's buried remains.
Bradley was a 16-year-old from Pennsylvania, trafficked to Boston; her family thought she might be overseas.
